projectDescription: A minimal Linux operating system that boots entirely into RAM, leaving no trace on storage after shutdown.
keyFeatures: Full RAM execution — the entire OS runs in memory with no disk dependency
No disk traces — shutdown leaves zero forensic footprint
Minimal initramfs — lightweight CPIO archive with BusyBox 1.35.0
Custom Linux kernel 6.16.1 compiled from source
Built from scratch using Linux From Scratch methodology
Automatic network configuration via DHCP at boot
GRUB 2.12 bootloader with BIOS and EFI support
publicApiSurface: scripts/build-initramfs.sh — builds the initramfs CPIO archive
/init — shell script that bootstraps the RAM environment
GRUB kernel params: init=/init rw console=tty1 [debug]
BusyBox utilities: sh, mount, ip, ping, vi, tar, grep, udhcpc, wget, and more
